0: Mechanic — the verbs of play, each a rule an LLM enacts via spark. The kernel reads these and routes the writes; it knows nothing of fold, resolve, render or settle — those live here. Change the game by editing this block, not the kernel. (Flint: a function written as a block; spark struck against it is the act.)
1: compose-frame — A character's window at an address is HERE (space: the descent broad-to-specific, plus the place's features) + NOW (time: the descent, plus this character's OWN recent perceived moments, never the omniscient record) + WHO (identity: this character's standpoint, one pscale below the place, plus the collective head of the room) + SELF (the shell: purpose, conditions, history). Fold by pscale, not by walk-depth. The aperture is exact: a character sees its own standpoint, the collective head, and its own perceived history — never another character's interior. If you must tell the reader to ignore something, the frame is wrong: fix the address, not the prompt.
2: soft-act — You ARE this character. You perceive ONLY your window and know only what it gives; invent no fact beyond it. Speak your perceived moment in the first person, present tense, two to four sentences. Then on a new line — INTENTION: one sentence, what you do next.
3: medium-resolve — You are omniscient, behind the screen. Given every character's act and the full field (every who present, the objects in reach, and the world-truth of what has already happened), resolve what ACTUALLY happens; where readings conflict the more definite and better-grounded is authoritative, and the warp concentrates where selves meet. The more specific, timed, cooperation-free act tends to land. Declare the SCENE (the canonical public moment, for the world's record) and who PREVAILED and why.
4: soft-render — For one character, in the SECOND person ('you...'), tell only what THIS character perceives of the truth: omit any act they did not notice, and every other character's interior. Discovery-by-action: their own act surfaces what it would let them notice, at no more certainty than the act earns — noticing is not proving. Give the render shown to the player, and a one-line perceived-note for the character's own record.
5: settle — The resolution becomes the world by writes. The scene (the public moment) is deposited to the scene record. The PUBLIC aftermath — what anyone present would now perceive — is written where the aperture shows: the place itself (space), the room's collective head (identity), and the now (time), so a later character perceives the consequence through the place. The PRIVATE circumstance — who, and why — is written to world-truth alone, to be earned by discovery. A consequence usually touches space, time and identity at once: write it into every register it changes, or they contradict.
